title: TESS-AP: an autonomous procedure to analyze TESS light curves
author: Khalack, V.
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The TESS space telescope performs photometric observation of the whole celestial sphere and provides data for more than 10 5  relatively bright stars. To succeed with the analysis of this extensive amount of data, we have created a high-performance autonomous computing procedure (TESS-AP) that carries out periodic analysis of the obtained light curves, detects stellar pulsations and selects stars with desired type of photometric variability. This procedure also performs an automatic search for known characteristics of studied stars in several databases like TIC, SIMBAD, GALAH, etc., and includes the derived data and respective references to the final report. It will help validate the stellar classification of the observed objects, as they appear in existing catalogues of variable stars.

To succeed with the analysis of this extensive amount of data, we have created a high-performance autonomous computing procedure (TESS-AP) that carries out periodic analysis of the obtained light curves, detects stellar pulsations and selects stars with desired type of photometric variability. This procedure also performs an automatic search for known characteristics of studied stars in several databases like TIC, SIMBAD, GALAH, etc., and includes the derived data and respective references to the final report. It will help validate the stellar classification of the observed objects, as they appear in existing catalogues of variable stars.
